Documentation

🚀Getting Started📦Installation🔄Transactions & Async✏️CRUD Operations🔍Querying🤖Source Generators📡Change Data Capture🌍Spatial Indexing🏗️Architecture🆔Custom ID Converters⚡Benchmarks🔌Schema-less API🔎BLQL📈TimeSeries🗝️Key-Value Store

🗝️ Embedded Key-Value Store

BLite 3.2.0 ships a persistent key-value store co-located in the same database file — no extra process, no extra file, no separate daemon. Access it via IBLiteKvStore on any BLiteEngine or DocumentDbContext.

📌 How it works

The KV engine uses a dedicated PageType.KeyValue = 13 page chain stored alongside your document collections. A compact in-memory index is rebuilt at startup and keeps all lookups O(1). Writes, deletes, and batch operations are serialized through a single write lock — ACID-safe without blocking concurrent document reads.

Installation

The KV store is built into BLite.Core — no extra NuGet package required.

dotnet add package BLite

Basic Usage

using BLite.Core;
using BLite.Core.KeyValue;

using var engine = new BLiteEngine("data.db");
IBLiteKvStore kv = engine.KvStore;

// Write — optional TTL per entry
kv.Set("session:abc", Encoding.UTF8.GetBytes("payload"), TimeSpan.FromHours(1));

// Read — returns null if absent or expired
byte[]? value = kv.Get("session:abc");

// Check / Delete
bool exists = kv.Exists("session:abc");
kv.Delete("session:abc");

// Extend the expiry without rewriting the value
kv.Refresh("session:abc", TimeSpan.FromHours(2));

Prefix Scan

// All keys starting with "session:"
IEnumerable<string> sessionKeys = kv.ScanKeys("session:");

// Every key in the store
IEnumerable<string> allKeys = kv.ScanKeys();

Atomic Batches

Batch() groups multiple set/delete operations under a single lock acquisition. All operations succeed or none do.

int applied = kv.Batch()
    .Set("k1", data1)
    .Set("k2", data2, TimeSpan.FromMinutes(30))
    .Delete("k3")
    .Execute();   // returns number of operations applied

Options

Pass BLiteKvOptions to the engine or context constructor:

var kvOptions = new BLiteKvOptions
{
    DefaultTtl         = TimeSpan.FromDays(1),  // applied when no explicit TTL is given
    PurgeExpiredOnOpen = true               // sweep on startup
};

// With BLiteEngine
using var engine = new BLiteEngine("data.db", kvOptions);

// With DocumentDbContext
using var db = new MyDbContext("app.db", kvOptions);

Using with DocumentDbContext

public partial class MyDbContext : DocumentDbContext
{
    public DocumentCollection<ObjectId, User> Users { get; set; } = null!;

    public MyDbContext(string path) : base(path) => InitializeCollections();
    public MyDbContext(string path, BLiteKvOptions kv) : base(path, kv) => InitializeCollections();
}

using var db = new MyDbContext("app.db");
db.KvStore.Set("last-sync", BitConverter.GetBytes(DateTime.UtcNow.Ticks));

Purge Expired Entries

Expired entries are soft-deleted on first access, so reads never return stale data. PurgeExpired() does a full-page sweep and reclaims wasted disk space.

int removed = kv.PurgeExpired();

BLite.Caching — IDistributedCache

BLite.Caching wraps the embedded KV store as a fully compliant IDistributedCache. Drop it in anywhere you would use Redis or SQL Server cache — zero external dependencies.

dotnet add package BLite.Caching

Register in ASP.NET Core

// Program.cs
builder.Services.AddBLiteDistributedCache("cache.db");

// With options
builder.Services.AddBLiteDistributedCache("cache.db", new BLiteKvOptions
{
    DefaultTtl         = TimeSpan.FromMinutes(30),
    PurgeExpiredOnOpen = true
});

Standard IDistributedCache

// Inject IDistributedCache — works with any existing caching code
await cache.SetStringAsync("key", "value", new DistributedCacheEntryOptions
{
    SlidingExpiration = TimeSpan.FromMinutes(20)
});
string? value = await cache.GetStringAsync("key");

Typed helpers (IBLiteCache)

Inject IBLiteCache for typed get/set via System.Text.Json and a thundering-herd-safe GetOrSetAsync backed by per-key SemaphoreSlim.

// Typed set
await cache.SetAsync("user:42", myUser, new DistributedCacheEntryOptions
{
    AbsoluteExpirationRelativeToNow = TimeSpan.FromHours(1)
});

// Typed get
User? user = await cache.GetAsync<User>("user:42");

// GetOrSet — stampede-safe factory
User user = await cache.GetOrSetAsync<User>("user:42",
    factory: async ct => await db.LoadUserAsync(42, ct),
    options: new DistributedCacheEntryOptions
    {
        SlidingExpiration = TimeSpan.FromMinutes(20)
    });
📌 Note

IBLiteCache is a superset of IDistributedCache. The same AddBLiteDistributedCache() registration satisfies both interfaces — inject whichever fits your code.

BLite Studio

The Key-Value Store panel in BLite Studio lets you browse, inspect, create, and delete entries without writing code. A Purge Expired button reclaims disk space on demand.

API Reference

MethodDescription
Get(key)Returns the raw bytes for key, or null if absent or expired.
Set(key, value, ttl?)Writes a value. Optional TTL overrides BLiteKvOptions.DefaultTtl.
Delete(key)Removes the entry. Returns true if found.
Exists(key)Returns true if the key exists and has not expired.
Refresh(key, ttl)Extends the expiry without rewriting the value.
ScanKeys(prefix="")Enumerates all live keys with the given prefix.
PurgeExpired()Reclaims storage used by expired entries. Returns count removed.
Batch()Returns a KvBatch builder for atomic multi-key operations.
